ai_summary: Havells India Limited reported robust consolidated revenue growth of nearly 20% year-on-year for Q1 FY27, reaching Rs 6,518.19 crores. However, net profit significantly declined by 16.6% year-on-year and a substantial 60% quarter-on-quarter to Rs 289.71 crores. This sharp profit contraction is primarily due to the absence of a large, non-recurring fair value gain on investment that boosted the previous quarter's results, coupled with increased raw material, traded goods, and advertising expenses, while the Lloyd Consumer segment continues to incur losses.
